methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Scout AI, a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Colby Adcock and Collin Otis, has raised $15M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies—2 public and 8 private—spanning the same sector and adjacent funding stages, including companies such as Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Tenna Systems.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $15M raised as a proxy for post-money value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage step-ups, yields $38M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $300M, with a range from $60M (Tenna Systems, Seed) to $29.0B (Scale AI, Series A). The peer range reflects meaningful dispersion in the defense-tech market, with the median driven by mid-stage Series A valuations ($150M–$500M).

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $169M. The wide range reflects two principal uncertainties: the substantial variance in peer valuations within Defense & Security, and the stage-gap between Scout AI (Seed) and most comparable companies (primarily Series A). Investors should weight the stage-progression result ($38M) more heavily as a conservative floor given Scout AI's earlier maturity relative to peers.
